accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text; references to DEA coordination, NIST AI RMF, and DHS Homeland Threat Assessment are correct.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as directing DHS Science and Technology Directorate to develop improved drug detection capabilities.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Accurately describes the amendment to Section 302 of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 adding paragraph (15).

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the rule of construction that nothing limits existing agency authority.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the December 23, 2024 approval date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all three technology categories, DEA coordination, NIST AI RMF requirement, threat assessment prioritization, and rule of construction.

correct_entities.why: DHS, DEA, NIST, Homeland Threat Assessment, State and Territory Report all correctly identified.
